idea: Build StockSignal, a deterministic inventory replenishment calculator for fictional plant-maintenance parts. Planners adjust demand, lead time, and safety stock and immediately see reorder points, recommended quantities, and priority ranking.
audience: Plant inventory planners and maintenance-material coordinators
outcome: Planners can understand which parts require action and why, while invalid assumptions are caught before they affect a recommendation.
constraints: Run locally with no backend, authentication, database, analytics, external APIs, or runtime network calls
Use fictional deterministic seed data and provide a visible reset action
Document the reorder-point and recommended-quantity formulas in the interface
Be responsive, keyboard operable, visibly focused, and accessible without color-only status
Keep the primary demo under three minutes
must_have_capabilities: Select a maintenance part and edit weekly demand, lead time, and safety stock
Calculate reorder point and recommended quantity deterministically
Reject empty, negative, nonnumeric, and unreasonable inputs with actionable messages
Re-rank parts when valid assumptions change
Reset all values to deterministic seeded defaults
visual_direction: A precise planning workbench with readable numbers, compact comparison tables, clear formulas, and calm industrial styling.
demo_preferences: Select a part, change demand and lead time, show its reorder point and ranking change, trigger and recover from an invalid input, then reset.
